audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for wilderness grumbling, manna/quail, daily portion, Sabbath rhythm, omer memorial.

verses.restored: They journeyed from Elim, and all congregation of children of Israel came to wilderness of Sin, between Elim and Sinai, on fifteenth day of second month after their going out from land of Egypt.
All congregation of children of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.

Children of Israel said to them, If only we had died by hand of YHWH in land of Egypt, when we sat by pots of flesh and ate bread to fullness; for you brought us out to this wilderness to kill all this assembly with hunger.
YHWH said to Moses, Look, I am raining bread for you from heavens; the people shall go out and gather a day's matter in its day, so I may test them whether they walk in my Torah/instruction or not.
On sixth day, when they prepare what they bring in, it shall be double what they gather day by day.
Moses and Aaron said to all children of Israel, Evening, then you shall know that YHWH brought you out from land of Egypt.
And morning, then you shall see glory of YHWH, because he hears your grumblings against YHWH. And what are we, that you grumble against us?

Moses said, When YHWH gives you flesh in evening to eat and bread in morning to fullness, because YHWH hears your grumblings that you grumble against him; what are we? Your grumblings are not against us but against YHWH.
Moses said to Aaron, Say to all congregation of children of Israel, Draw near before YHWH, for he has heard your grumblings.
As Aaron spoke to all congregation of children of Israel, they turned toward wilderness, and look, glory of YHWH appeared in cloud.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

I have heard grumblings of children of Israel. Speak to them, saying, Between the evenings you shall eat flesh, and in morning you shall be satisfied with bread; and you shall know that I am YHWH your Divine.
It happened in evening that quail came up and covered the camp, and in morning there was layer of dew around the camp.
The layer of dew went up, and look, upon face of wilderness, a fine flaky thing, fine like frost on earth.
Children of Israel saw and said each to one's brother, What is it? For they did not know what it was. Moses said to them, It is bread that YHWH has given you to eat.
This is the word that YHWH commanded: Gather from it, each according to one's eating, an omer per head, according to number of your souls/lives; each shall take for those in one's tent.
Children of Israel did so, and they gathered, some more and some less.
They measured by omer; the one gathering much had no excess, and the one gathering little had no lack. Each gathered according to one's eating.
Moses said to them, Let no one leave any of it until morning.
But they did not listen to Moses; some left from it until morning, and it bred worms and stank. Moses was angry with them.
They gathered it morning by morning, each according to one's eating; when sun grew hot, it melted.
On sixth day they gathered double bread, two omers for one; all leaders of congregation came and told Moses.
He said to them, This is what YHWH spoke: Tomorrow is Sabbath-rest, holy Sabbath to YHWH. Bake what you bake and boil what you boil, and all surplus set aside for yourselves to keep until morning.
They set it aside until morning, as Moses commanded, and it did not stink, and worm was not in it.
Moses said, Eat it today, for today is Sabbath to YHWH; today you will not find it in field.
Six days you shall gather it, but on seventh day, Sabbath, there will be none in it.
It happened on seventh day some of the people went out to gather, but they did not find.
YHWH said to Moses, How long do you refuse to keep my commands and my Torah/instructions?
See, because YHWH has given you Sabbath, therefore on sixth day he gives you bread of two days; sit each under oneself; let no one go out from one's place on seventh day.
So the people rested on seventh day.
House of Israel called its name manna; it was like coriander seed, white, and its taste like wafers with honey.

Moses said, This is the word YHWH commanded: Fill an omer from it to keep throughout your generations, so they may see the bread with which I fed you in wilderness when I brought you out from land of Egypt.
Moses said to Aaron, Take one jar and put there a full omer of manna, and place it before YHWH to keep throughout your generations.
As YHWH commanded Moses, Aaron placed it before the testimony to keep.
Children of Israel ate the manna forty years, until they came to inhabited land; they ate the manna until they came to border of land of Canaan.
Now an omer is a tenth of an ephah.

translation_notes: Manna asks 'what is it?' and trains daily dependence rather than hoarding.
The omer equality line is central: much had no excess, little had no lack.
Sabbath appears as gift before Sinai law code: rest is liberation practice.
safety_note: Do not frame grumbling as simple moral failure; hunger and wilderness insecurity are real. The chapter forms trust through provision, equality, and Sabbath rest.
